_meta.author: Claude (national autopilot — CA federal batch)
_meta.note: CA federal (4 CA-48 challengers, all D). HEADLINE WIN: 4 CA-48 candidates leave active pool — Campa-Najjar / Riker / Nash lost the June 2 2026 top-two primary (Desmond R 41.6% and von Wilpert D 19.7% advance); Dixit withdrew 2025-11-19 after Prop 50 redistricting moved his Coachella base out of CA-48. NOTE: Mike Thompson (CA-04 incumbent, evidence_federal ready — chairs Gun Violence Prevention Task Force, RFMA + Equality Act co-author, pro-choice Catholic, voted NO on Born-Alive, TRUE on Article I war powers + AUMF repeal) was researched but DEFERRED — slug `mike-thompson` collides across CA US Rep + KS State Senator + MS State Senator records and the engine resolves by slug only. Disambiguation (e.g. rename CA to `mike-thompson-ca-04`) required before refining; left in queue for the next pass.

profile.background: 2026 D candidate for U.S. House CA-48 (San Diego / Coachella). Prior 2018 + 2020 D nominee for CA-50. LOST the June 2 2026 top-two primary (~9.5%, 3rd place); publicly retired from politics ('my political career is permanently over').

ammar-campa-najjar.notes_set: Ammar Campa-Najjar — 2026 CA-48 D primary candidate. [2026-06-05 national autopilot, federal tier] LOST the June 2 2026 top-two primary with ~9.5% (3rd place); Desmond (R, 41.6%) and von Wilpert (D, 19.7%) advance to November. Conceded the night of June 2: 'While my political career is permanently over, I'm deeply grateful to the voters.' Blamed pro-Israel super-PAC spending ($5M+, including Democratic Majority for Israel) for the loss. Documented positions before exit: campaign listed 'Reproductive Freedom' as a top-five priority (sanctity_of_life FALSE); 'Dismantling ICE' as a headline (border FALSE [2]); 'Ending the Forever Wars' as a headline (CROSS-PARTY credit on foreign_policy_restraint [1]).

0.note: 2026 campaign site lists 'Reproductive Freedom' as a top-five priority — public pro-choice posture.

1.note: Reproductive-Freedom priority; no abortion-abolition advocacy — opposite of personhood/abolition.

2.note: Campaign headlines 'Dismantling ICE' — explicitly pro-sanctuary / anti-federal-immigration-enforcement.

1.note: Campaign-site headline 'Ending the Forever Wars' — CROSS-PARTY credit on forever-war withdrawal posture.

profile.religion: Undisclosed (Indian-American background; no public profession of faith located)
profile.background: 2026 D candidate for U.S. House CA-48 (originally launched CA-41 vs. Calvert, switched to CA-48 after Issa retirement). USAF veteran (6 yrs), voting-rights attorney (Columbia Law, ex-WilmerHale), openly gay. WITHDREW 2025-11-19 after Prop 50 redistricting moved his Coachella Valley base out of the new CA-48.

anuj-dixit.notes_set: Anuj Dixit — 2026 CA-48 D primary candidate. [2026-06-05 national autopilot, federal tier] WITHDREW from the race on 2025-11-19, citing Proposition 50 redistricting which moved his Riverside County / Coachella Valley home base out of the new CA-48. Did NOT appear on the June 2 2026 primary ballot. Campaign site (anujdixitforcongress.com) is offline. Documented identity-based evidence on marriage cells: openly gay candidate who ran in part on becoming the 'first LGBTQ Indian American elected to Congress.' No detailed policy planks were published before withdrawal — most cells left null per the no-inference rule.

0.note: Openly gay candidate; campaigned on becoming the first LGBTQ Indian American in Congress — opposite of one-man-one-woman affirmation.

1.note: Same LGBTQ-first framing — incompatible with opposing same-sex marriage in law.

profile.background: 2026 D candidate for U.S. House CA-48 (Palm Springs-based economist, former Obama 2008 organizer, SafePlace International board member for LGBTQ refugees, openly gay). LSE-trained. LOST the June 2 2026 top-two primary (~6.7%, 5th place).

brandon-riker.notes_set: Brandon Riker — 2026 CA-48 D primary candidate. [2026-06-05 national autopilot, federal tier] LOST the June 2 2026 top-two primary with ~6.7% (5th place); Desmond (R) and von Wilpert (D) advance. Documented positions: pledged to codify Roe (sanctity_of_life FALSE); openly gay, pledged to pass the Equality Act, direct quote 'I will always stand with the trans community' and called gender-affirming care 'medically necessary' (biblical_marriage FALSE across the board); endorsed by Gun Sense Voters gun-control PAC (self_defense FALSE); pledged the John R. Lewis VRA Advancement Act (election_integrity [1] FALSE); pledged to split CBP into two branches and 'hold ICE accountable' (border FALSE [1]).

0.note: Pledged to codify Roe v. Wade into law — opposite of personhood at conception.

1.note: Roe codification pledge — opposite of abortion abolition.

0.note: Openly gay candidate; Equality Act pledge — opposite of one-man-one-woman affirmation.

1.note: Equality Act pledge codifies same-sex marriage protections in federal civil-rights law.

2.note: Direct quote: 'I will always stand with the trans community'; called gender-affirming care 'medically necessary.'

4.note: Equality Act pledge + SafePlace International LGBTQ-refugee board — promotes LGBTQ identity in federal policy.

1.note: Pledged the John R. Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act — federal voter-ID preclearance, opposite of photo voter ID + citizenship verification.
KPBS interview: pledged to split CBP into two branches and 'hold ICE accountable' — opposite of mandatory-deportation posture.

0.note: Endorsed by Gun Sense Voters gun-control PAC — opposite of constitutional-carry posture.

1.note: Gun Sense Voter endorsement requires support for red-flag laws / assault-weapons bans / mag limits.

profile.background: 2026 D candidate for U.S. House CA-48 (Murrieta / Riverside County, Bank of America process-design consultant, MBA Indiana U). Prior LOST 2022 CA State Senate race to R Kelly Seyarto 62-38. LOST the June 2 2026 CA-48 top-two primary (long-shot fourth-Dem entrant; outside top 2).

brian-nash-ca-48.notes_set: Brian Nash — 2026 CA-48 D primary candidate. [2026-06-05 national autopilot, federal tier] LOST the June 2 2026 top-two primary (long-shot fourth-Dem entrant from Riverside County; outside the top 2 — Desmond R and von Wilpert D advance). Documented positions from his 2022 CA State Senate run (no detailed 2026 federal positions published): publicly pro-choice ('not my place as a politician to tell a woman what to do'; called Dobbs 'appalling'); publicly pro-gun-restriction ('I want to help write laws governing guns in California'; 'supports stricter gun regulations'). Marriage / family / Christian liberty / border / foreign-policy / industry-capture cells left null — no documented positions located.

0.note: Direct quote: 'I believe is not my place as a politician to tell a woman what to do' — pro-choice posture, opposite of personhood at conception.

1.note: Called the Dobbs ruling 'appalling… not in line with the majority of people' — opposite of abortion abolition.

0.note: Direct quote: 'I want to help write laws governing guns in California. I want to challenge the status quo' — opposite of constitutional carry.

1.note: Supports stricter gun regulations — opposite of opposing red-flag laws, AWBs, and registries.
